ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY WITH REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF
ORDER - STIPULATION AND ORDER FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY WITH REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF CHEONG KONG IS ORDERED TO SERVE CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES WITHOUT OBJECTIONS TO DR. CHI'S FORM INTERROGATORIES-SET ONE, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES-SET ONE, REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS-SET ONE, REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS-SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR STATEMENT OF DAMAGES WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE ORDER. FURTHER, THE TRUTH OF THE MATTERS SPECIFIED IN THE REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS IS DEEMED ADMITTED UNLESS A PROPOSED RESPONSE TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION THAT IS IN SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE WITH SECTION 2033.220 IS SERVED BEFORE THE HEARING ON THE MOTION WITH SATIFACTORY PROOF TO THE COURT. MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $645.00 ARE AWARDED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: DENNIS HYDE; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-14-2019 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance, the Court ordered the case dismissed pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Sections 583.210 and 583.250. The court takes judicial notice of the three-year statute of limitations. All previously imposed sanctions against the plaintiff are vacated. Judge: GARRETT L. WONG; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Proceedings not reported. (610)

Off Calendar May-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Emmanuel Enyinwa, Esq., Law Offices of Emmanuel Enyinwa, appearing for Plaintiff;
Gregory Jordan Lewis, Esq., Hassard Bonnington LLP, Telephone 415-288-9800, appearing for Defendant(s) Douglas Hanks et al;

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS THIENKHAI VU, M.D., DOUGLAS HANKS, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants Thienkhai Vu, M.D. and Douglas Hanks, M.D.'s demurrer to the first amended complaint is ordered OFF CALENDAR as moot due to granting of their CCP 583.210 motion to dismiss.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Emmanuel Enyinwa, Esq., Law Offices of Emmanuel Enyinwa, appearing for Plaintiff;
Gregory Jordan Lewis, Esq., Hassard Bonnington LLP, Telephone 415-288-9800, appearing for Defendant(s) Douglas Hanks et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT THIENKHAI VU, M.D., DOUGLAS HANKS, M.D. Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint

Matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

**After hearing, an order granting defendants Thienkhai Vu M.D. and Douglas Hanks, M.D.'s motion to dismiss plaintiff's frist amended complaint is signed.**

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment By Defendant Thomas Chi, M.D.
Off Calendar Nov-22-2016 Continued To Dec-21-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery With Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar May-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jami B. Tencati, of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. 415.288.9800, for defendants Dr. Samuel Chi;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. DEFENDANT THOMAS CHI, M.D. Motion To Compel Discovery With Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff is granted. Plaintiff Cheong Kong is ordered to serve code-compliant responses without objections to Dr. Chi's Form Interrogatories-Set One, Special Interrogatories-Set One, Requests for Production of Documents-Set One, Request for Admissions-Set One, and Request for Statement of Damages within ten (10) days of notice of entry of the order. Further, the truth of the matters specified in the Request for Admissions is deemed admitted unless a proposed response to the requests for admission that is in substantial compliance with Section 2033.220 is served before the hearing on the motion with satifactory proof to the court. Monetary Sanctions of $645.00 are awarded. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Dennis Hyde; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
